items.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia

children.content: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after being slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

gallery.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

thumbnail.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

items.title: Tourism operator at coalface of bird flu response feels unprepared

children.content: A tourism operator on South Australia's coast is frustrated by what he says is poor communication from authorities about bird flu. He says he saw sick birds weeks ago but did not know to report the sighting.

image.altText: A giant petrel bird hides in green seaside foliage at Knights Beach on the Fleurieu Peninsula.

items.title: 'We don't fully understand': The footy risk few parents grasp

children.content: Contact is an essential part of footy, but there is growing evidence it can also be a grave threat to the health and welfare of its players. Experts say the AFL should be acting now to protect current and future players.

image.altText: Players compete for the ball at a junior Australian Rules football match.

avatar.alt: Portrait of ABC journalist Jessica Halloran.

items.title: Randall says concussion left her 'no choice' but to retire from AFLW

children.content: Adelaide Crows star Chelsea Randall announces her retirement from the AFLW, citing concussion as a major factor in her decision to hang up her boots and step away from the game.

gallery.altText: Adelaide Crows AFLW star Chelsea Randall.

thumbnail.altText: Adelaide Crows AFLW star Chelsea Randall.

items.title: Power tools used in 'stupid' and potentially lethal acts of Stobie pole vandalism

children.content: Members of the public were put at "severe risk" of electrocution after four Stobie poles were vandalised in Adelaide's north in the past month, causing some to fall and expose high voltage electrical lines.

image.altText: A damaged Stobie pole lies on the ground near a popular walking and mountain bike track in the Cobbler Creek Recreation Park

items.title: Thousands of outback roads damaged by floods could take year to fix

children.content: Damage to South Australia's network of outback roads is isolating remote communities, preventing children from getting to school, and slowing the restocking process for pastoralists recovering from drought.

image.altText: A man holding a tape measure down the edge of a washed out dirt road

items.title: Four fatal crashes in 12 days rock SA's Limestone Coast

children.content: Police are calling on drivers to stay attentive and slow down after four people were killed on one region's roads in just 12 days.

image.altText: A bunch of flowers placed next to a road.

items.title: Family who spent $250,000 on IVF welcomes new rebate scheme in SA

children.content: A South Australian couple who spent $250,000 on fertility treatments say the government's IVF rebate will make a "huge difference" for people trying to conceive.

image.altText: A man holds a toddler while a woman speaks into media microphones outside a house

items.title: Network 10 strikes last-minute deal to extend broadcasting in SA and NSW

children.content: Two regions in South Australia and one in New South Wales were set to lose access to free-to-air Network 10 services in coming days, but a new agreement means coverage will continue until the end of September.

image.altText: Sign of the number 10 in a blue circle on a tower with trees visible

items.title: Ageing 'Nashos' call for priority as all veterans get equal access to health benefits

children.content: All Australian veterans will soon have access to claim compensation for health issues, fulfilling a royal commission recommendation, but those conscripted during the Vietnam War say their advanced age means their claims should receive priority.

image.altText: An old photo of young soldiers in a casual setting

images.altText: Gustavo Gomez screams in delight and runs off in celebration
Germany players react as a Paraguay player celebrates in the foreground.
Orlando Gill screams towards the crowd while players celebrate behind him

articles.title: How a punt on a trial in Italy created the Socceroos' next star

children.content: Cristian Volpato was rejected by two Australian soccer academies. His family dropped everything to go for a trial in Italy. That was the making of Australia's next star.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato #20 of Australia strikes the ball during the FIFA World Cup

children.content: Paraguay knocks Germany out of the 2026 World Cup, beating the four-time world champions Germany in a penalty shootout, while Brazil's Gabriel Martinelli scores in the 96th minute to break Japanese hearts. Follow live.

items.title: Paraguay knocks Germany out after incredible penalty shootout drama

updates.title: Germany to kick first in the shootout
Germany and Paraguay head to penalties

items.title: Social media giants face fines for failing to hand over documents

children.content: Labor wants to double the fines for tech giants who refuse to hand over documents that could prove they are failing to enforce Australia’s social media age ban.

image.altText: A woman in an burnt orange suit, gestures with her hands while standing at a lectern.

items.title: Multiple injuries, driver unaccounted for in truck-stop crash inferno

children.content: Explosions and multiple injuries as up to seven semi-trailers are destroyed after trucks collide on the Hume Highway then veer into a rest area near Goulburn.

image.altText: rfs truck with blaze behind it on highway road

items.title: Sons remember 'loving' mum after Airbnb guest found guilty of killing her

children.content: Maree Vermont's sons say they never thought hosting Airbnb guests would be dangerous for their mother, who was proud of the home she had built.

image.altText: Two men stand in front of a stone house with signs of fire damage.

items.title: Ill-fated midnight mountain walk in freezing conditions led to 12-day search

children.content: The NSW coroner praises emergency services for "persistence" during the 12-day search for a hiker who set off on an ill-fated midnight mountain walk in sub-zero temperatures while wearing sandals.

image.altText: A woman with blond hair smiling at the camera.

items.title: Multi-million-dollar giveaway club banned by NSW regulator

children.content: One of Australia's biggest "rewards clubs" has been banned from running any gaming activity in New South Wales for 18 months after allegedly breaking the law.

gallery.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

thumbnail.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

items.title: ASX headed for cautious start, Wall Street jumps as AI-related stocks rebound

children.content: The Australian share market is likely to start its day relatively flat despite a rebound in oil prices ahead of the next round of US-Iran peace talks. On Wall Street, the Dow Jones Industrial Average hit a record high. Follow live.

image.altText: Picture of stock market board with ASX sign at the top left corner.

items.title: BalletTok, blackface and nudes: Inside the world of professional ballet

children.content: Ariane Beeston, the author of an acclaimed memoir detailing her experience with postpartum psychosis, has penned her first work of fiction, Don't Forget to Smile.

image.altText: Woman with long dark hair sitting wearing white and looking at camera

items.title: More Australians are doing their own tax returns and making these mistakes

children.content: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.

gallery.altText: woman looking at laptop on the couch with paperwork

thumbnail.altText: woman looking at laptop on the couch with paperwork

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: Will you raise the white flag on these tricky flag questions?

children.content: How many stars are there on the US flag? And how many stars are on the Europe flag?

image.altText: The Australian, Aboriginal and Torres Strait flags fly in Canberra.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz strikes likely to disrupt shipping again

children.content: Fresh attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz have exposed the fragility of the 60-day ceasefire deal between the US and Iran, which was meant to allow ships to transit through the waterway again.

image.altText: A drone view of vessles anchored in the Strait of Hormuz as seen from Musandam, Oman

items.title: NDIS shell company boom sparks crackdown

children.content: The federal government says it is moving to crack down on the growing trade in "off-the-shelf" NDIS providers.

gallery.altText: A middle aged white woman with short fair hair and red suit sitting in an office chair

thumbnail.altText: A middle aged white woman with short fair hair and red suit sitting in an office chair

items.title: Court rejects Trump's final bid to overturn E Jean Carroll verdict

children.content: The US Supreme Court declined to hear US President Donald Trump's bid to overturn a $5 million USD verdict in a case in which a jury found him liable for sexually abusing and defaming E Jean Carroll.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and orange tinted glasses

items.title: Homemade production of 'lethal weapons' on the rise

children.content: Since Queensland strengthened its firearm laws in March, one person a fortnight has been charged with possessing a blueprint to make 3D-printed guns.

image.altText: an open shoulder bag with several glocks inside

items.title: Bondi terror attack survivor received 'influx of hate' hours after shooting

children.content: Deepfake and altered images depicting a Bondi Beach terror attack survivor holding an Academy Award while covered in blood spread "like wildfire" online just hours after the shooting, a royal commission has heard.

gallery.altText: Arsen Ostrovsky in a suit and blue tie outside a building

thumbnail.altText: Arsen Ostrovsky in a suit and blue tie outside a building

items.title: Police hold 'real concern' about anonymous letters sent to politicians

children.content: Police have asked for the community's assistance to identify the author targeting parliamentarians and religious organisations, who goes by 'Scorpio' or 'Bullit'.

image.altText: A composite of a scary face with 'Scorpio' written underneath and capital letters underlined in red on the right.

items.title: ABC presenter Trevor Chappell to hang up mic after 26 years

children.content: After decades of keeping night owls and shift workers company on ABC radio across Australia, Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell decides to call it a day at the age of 65.

image.altText: Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell wears a blue shirt and stands near the arts centre

items.title: PM-in-waiting Andy Burnham lays out plan to fix 'broken' British politics

children.content: Andy Burnham, the British MP tipped to replace the outgoing prime minister Sir Keir Starmer, promised "radical change" to the nation's "broken" political system as he laid out his vision to lead the country on Monday.

gallery.altText: A man with a suit jacket on speaking at a lecturn and gesturing with his right hand.

thumbnail.altText: A man with a suit jacket on speaking at a lecturn and gesturing with his right hand.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: New measures coming to NSW include end to 'one of the biggest rip-offs'

children.content: Motorists, public transport users and wage earners are the biggest winners under new changes across NSW starting from July 1.

image.altText: A wallet with an NAB and Qantas card and a $5 and $10 notes poking out

items.title: Australia helping seven in Venezuela as earthquake death toll rises

children.content: Australia provides "consular assistance" to seven people after the Venezuela's two earthquakes, Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong has confirmed.

image.altText: an aerial image of apartment buildings reduced to rubble

items.title: The medicine safety system most Australians have never heard of

children.content: A little black triangle on medicine information leaflets is meant to make it easier to report adverse effects of new medications. Problem is, many Australians don't know what it means.

gallery.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

thumbnail.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

items.title: Former England captain says Brook can't lead Test side under McCullum

children.content: Ben Stokes says vice-captain Harry Brook has his "100 per cent" support to lead England's Test side, but coach Brendon McCullum is less effusive.

image.altText: Ben Stokes with his arm around Harry Brook during the national anthem.

items.title: Six killed in German 'family tragedy' shooting, say police

children.content: Two people have been arrested after a shooting in the northern German town of Stade.

image.altText: Crime scene techs stand next to an emergency services vehicle on cobbled street.

items.title: Osaka channels Kill Bill as Sinner and Sabalenka reverse French misery

children.content: Jannik Sinner is pushed to the brink and Aryna Sabalenka coasts into the second round as both top seeds bounce back from disappointment at the French Open.

articles.title: Australian Kasatkina finally wins on grass to kick off Wimbledon run

children.content: After a winless grass-court swing, Australian Daria Kasatkina becomes Australia's first winner at Wimbledon for 2026.

image.altText: Daria Kasatkina hits a forehand at Wimbledon.

Jannik Sinner shouts during a match at Wimbledon.
